title: Japan-DPRK summary: no missile activity in Japan EEZ as Tokyo monitors Iran fallout
summary: Japan-North Korea Bridge Summary reports that no DPRK missile activity affected Japanese territorial waters or EEZ during the past 24 hours, while Tokyo monitors Brent crude's spike to $126 per barrel and prepares contingency planning for the Iran blockade's impact on Japanese refiners.
contents:
# Japan-DPRK summary: no missile activity in Japan EEZ as Tokyo monitors Iran fallout

## DPRK Threat Picture
Japan-North Korea Bridge Summary's April 30 brief confirms no DPRK missile launches affected Japanese territorial waters or the Exclusive Economic Zone during the past 24 hours. Japan Air Self-Defense Force routine air policing operations continued without contact with North Korean assets. Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force vessels maintained scheduled patrol patterns in the Sea of Japan.

## Tokyo's Energy Calculus
The Japanese Cabinet Secretariat reportedly held coordination meetings with energy ministry officials and major Japanese refiners following the Brent crude spike to $126 per barrel. Pre-blockade, Iranian and Iran-linked dark-fleet crude flowed indirectly to Japanese refiners via Chinese, Indian or Korean intermediaries; the new OFAC Chinese refinery designation tightens the chain. Japan retains ample strategic petroleum reserves but faces medium-term replacement-sourcing pressure.

## Trilateral Coordination
JNB Summary notes that the US-Japan-ROK trilateral framework remains active for DPRK missile-tracking and information-sharing, with no observed gaps during the Iran-focused weeks. Japan's Self-Defense Forces continue to participate in scheduled exercises with US Forces Japan and the ROK military.
